evidence.snippet: Digital Lidar Sensors for Automation, Drones & Robotics | Ouster | Ouster
### Robotics and Drones
An ultra-wide field-of-view sensor designed for short-range, high-precision perception in robotics, warehouse automation, and industrial applications.
*   Ultra light weight for extended drone flight times and dual return mode for improved mapping capabilities, even in environments with small branches and wires.

items.notes: Lidar sensors (OS0, OS1, OS2, OSDome) explicitly marketed for Robotics and Drones verticals; OS0 page notes 'ultra light weight for extended drone flight times'

evidence.snippet: Ouster, Inc. is a corporation established in Delaware with a registered office at 350 Treat Avenue San Francisco, CA 94110, United States. Ouster has affiliates located in Europe, North America and Asia.
Digital Lidar Sensors for Automation, Drones & Robotics | Ouster | Ouster

Ouster’s high-resolution lidar sensors for long, mid, and short range applications — and our accompanying digital ecosystem — are enabling worldwide automation. Powering the next industrial revolution and a better future for the global workforce, our technology is in good hands with you.

drone_supply_chain_role.notes: Ouster sells lidar sensor components (OS0/1/2/OSDome) that drone builders integrate; drones are a named vertical but Ouster does not manufacture complete platforms

evidence.snippet:
OS0
===
An ultra-wide field-of-view sensor designed for short-range, high-precision perception in robotics, warehouse automation, and industrial applications.

Up to 5.2m points per second with 128 channels
of vertical resolution.
------------------------------------------------------------------------
Engineered for industrial, robotics, mapping, security, crowd analytics, traffic, and automotive applications.

OS2
===
A long-range sensor delivering high-resolution perception for large-scale infrastructure monitoring, security, and autonomous mobility.

======
OS Dome – A hemispherical lidar sensor with a 180º field of view, ideal for security, smart infrastructure, and autonomous system awareness.

VLP 16
======
Mid-range lidar sensor

VLS 128
=======
Long-range lidar sensor

items.name: Ouster Studio

evidence.snippet:
Ouster Studio
=============
Digital Lidar Visualizer and Data Management.
*   [NDAA compliant OS1](/insights/blog/ouster-os1-lidar-dod-approved-for-unmanned-aircraft-and-secure-government-operations)

ndaa.notes: Footer sitewide link explicitly labelled 'NDAA compliant OS1'; links to a blog post (skipped_class_budget) titled 'ouster-os1-lidar-dod-approved-for-unmanned-aircraft-and-secure-government-operations'. Claim applies to OS1 specifically; coverage for other SKUs not confirmed in available pages.

evidence.snippet: DoD-Approved OS1 Lidar for Unmanned Aircraft and Secure Government Operations

Ouster designs its digital lidar sensors with strong supply chain security in mind. The OS1's Blue UAS approval officially confirms this security and positions Ouster as a trusted supplier of 3D Lidar sensors for U.S. defense applications.

blue_uas.notes: OS1 is explicitly on the Blue UAS Framework managed by DIU; first high-resolution 3D lidar sensor included.

evidence.snippet:
Build faster with high-volume manufacturing and worldwide fulfillment.
----------------------------------------------------------------------
We offer a scalable outsourced manufacturing and operate local fulfillment in three major global regions, ensuring efficient order fulfillment, faster lead times, and lower total costs for all our lidar sensors production.

readiness.notes: Catalog SKUs with published specs and datasheets; site advertises high-volume manufacturing and worldwide regional fulfillment

unresolved_questions: Which specific NDAA section(s) (848 vs 889) the OS1 is certified under, and whether OS0/OS2/OSDome carry the same certification

fetch_requests.reason: Would confirm specific NDAA section (848/889) compliance details for OS1 and clarify whether other SKUs (OS0, OS2, OSDome) share the same compliance status

tagline: Digital lidar sensor and perception-software supplier. Produces OS series (OS0–OS2, OSDome) scanning lidars and VLP/VLS spinning variants ranging 35–400m; OS1 NDAA-compliant. Recently acquired Stereolabs stereo/mono cameras and ZED Box edge compute. Blue UAS status unclear. Software stack includes Gemini (perception middleware), BlueCity (smart-city traffic), and Studio (point-cloud visualization). Public evidence supports production readiness across drone, robotics, security, and ITS verticals; limited visible DoD program attribution.
